memory.summary: I could relax now, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. I'll probably procrastinate again until the last minute.

desires: Get that quick chore done immediately so I can feel a dopamine hit and stop thinking about it.
Avoid starting the term project now because the friction feels too high, even though I know I'll panic later.
Stay up late tonight to binge on social media or games with my friends instead of sleeping, justifying it as 'recharging' before Thursday.

goals.description: Execute the quick_chore immediately to get that instant dopamine hit and clear my head.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference craves immediate rewards over long-term gains. The friction of starting the term project is too high right now, so I'm prioritizing the easy win to regulate my mood before anything else.

goals.description: Scroll social media/games with cohort_3 for 2 hours instead of sleeping or working on the project.
goals.rationale: I need to recharge socially (closeness 7) to avoid stress, but my sleep discipline is poor. Justifying this as 'recharging' allows me to ignore the looming Thursday deadline without feeling guilty yet.

agent.last_reasoning: I need the instant dopamine hit to regulate my mood before tackling the hard project later. This clears a small task, but I'm skipping the term project now because starting it feels too friction-heavy right now. I'll just message cohort_3 later so they don't feel ignored while I slack off.
